01_initial_request.input_data: Gas multi-parameter time series data and historical observation data
01_initial_request.user_intent: To accurately predict coal mine gas concentration for early warning of gas disasters and reduce accidents by solving low prediction accuracy and long-term prediction limitations
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Higher prediction accuracy and evaluation indices closer to the real value

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Coal mine monitoring data has problems such as data loss and redundancy due to harsh underground environment. [Gap] Raw data cannot be directly used as model input features as it increases training burden and reduces prediction accuracy. [Decision] To achieve data quality improvement, chose to use Lagrange interpolation method to perform missing data filling on gas multi-parameter time series data, expecting to obtain complete dataset.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Missing data in the original dataset was filled, ensuring data integrity for model training

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] After filling missing data, the dataset still contains redundant features that affect model performance. [Gap] Redundant features increase training burden and reduce prediction accuracy. [Decision] To achieve feature dimensionality reduction, chose to use PCA (Principal Component Analysis) method to perform redundant feature elimination on the original dataset, expecting to obtain optimized feature set.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Redundant features were eliminated from the original dataset

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] After feature elimination, the time series data still has dimensional influence among gas multi-parameters. [Gap] Dimensional differences affect model training and prediction accuracy. [Decision] To achieve dimensional normalization, chose to use min-max normalization method to perform time series processing on gas multi-parameter time series, expecting to obtain normalized data without dimensional influence.

parameters.purpose: eliminate dimensional influence among gas multi-parameter time series

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Time series data was normalized to eliminate dimensional influence

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional gas concentration prediction models cannot meet long-term prediction demands of coal mine safety production. [Gap] Need to establish a framework that supports both trend and time point prediction. [Decision] To achieve long-term trend prediction capability, chose to use TP-TPP framework to perform data reconstruction under trend prediction by expanding time interval between data samples, expecting to obtain reconstructed input features reflecting central tendency, extreme value and dispersion degree.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Data samples were redivided with 10-minute intervals, calculating average, max, min, and variance as input features

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Time point prediction framework focuses on recent prediction accuracy for short-term emergency response. [Gap] Need to prepare data specifically for time point prediction with higher accuracy requirements. [Decision] To achieve short-term accurate prediction, chose to use TP-TPP framework to perform time point prediction data preparation by eliminating missing data and redundancy, expecting to obtain clean dataset for TPP framework.

parameters.operations: eliminate missing data and data redundancy

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Missing data and redundancy were eliminated for time point prediction framework

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Original Snake Optimizer algorithm has fixed parameter c2 that is difficult to match with individual evolution, leading to local optimum issues. [Gap] Small c2 value cannot jump out of local optimum; large c2 value leads to slower convergence. [Decision] To achieve improved global search ability, chose to use improved snake optimization algorithm with random disturbance strategy to perform parameter optimization, expecting to obtain enhanced search range and global optimization capability.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Random disturbance factors were introduced to improve search range of individuals and global search ability

references.relevance: Random disturbance strategy for improving search range

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Snake Optimizer still needs improvement to avoid premature convergence and enhance population diversity. [Gap] Algorithm may fall into local optimum without sufficient diversity. [Decision] To achieve enhanced population diversity and avoid local optimum, chose to use improved snake optimization algorithm with opposition learning strategy to perform worst value reset on individuals in decision layer, expecting to obtain improved global search ability.

parameters.improvement: opposition learning resets the worst value
parameters.target: worst solution individuals in snake group decision-making

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Worst solution individuals were reset through oppositional learning, improving population diversity and avoiding local optimum

references.relevance: Oppositional learning strategy for expanding search range and enhancing diversity

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional LSTM has independent gate units (forget, input, output) that may cause feature redundancy or insufficient feature extraction. [Gap] Input gate does not refer to forgetting information when updating cell state. [Decision] To achieve better time series feature extraction, chose to use SLSTM model with improved gating structure (select gate, forget gate, update gate) to perform feature extraction on gas concentration time series, expecting to obtain enhanced connection between gating units and reduced redundancy.

parameters.gate_structure: select gate, forget gate, update gate
parameters.target: gas concentration time series and other measurement points data

02_agent_trajectory.observation: New gated structural units were constructed with select gate filtering redundant information, enhancing connection between gating units

references.relevance: SLSTM model variant with improved gate structure

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single LSTM model cannot fully extract spatial and temporal characteristics for gas concentration prediction. [Gap] Need encoder-decoder architecture for better feature extraction and prediction. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive feature extraction and accurate prediction, chose to use Encoder-Decoder-SLSTM model with SLSTM as encoder and LSTM as decoder to perform gas concentration prediction, expecting to obtain context feature hidden vector and time series prediction results.

parameters.target: gas concentration value and environmental monitoring data

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Encoder-Decoder network structure was established with SLSTM encoder extracting time-related features and LSTM decoder generating prediction results

references.relevance: Encoder-Decoder framework for gas concentration prediction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Neural network parameters need optimization for best prediction performance. [Gap] Manual parameter adjustment is time-consuming and may not find optimal values. [Decision] To achieve optimal model parameters, chose to use improved SO algorithm to perform parameter adjustment on ED-SLSTM neural network, expecting to obtain simplified parameter adjustment process with higher convergence accuracy and speed.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Modified SO algorithm was used to optimize neural network parameters with improved convergence accuracy and speed

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multiple models need to be compared to verify ED-SLSTM prediction accuracy. [Gap] Need quantitative evaluation to demonstrate model superiority. [Decision] To achieve model performance verification, chose to use evaluation indexes (MAPE, RMSE, R²) to perform model evaluation and comparison against LightGBM, LSTM, Bi-LSTM, LSTM-Attention, and CEEMDAN-LSTM models, expecting to obtain quantitative metrics showing ED-SLSTM superiority.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ED-SLSTM showed higher prediction accuracy with MAPE within 5% in TPP framework and R² above 0.85 in TP framework at 6 prediction steps

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative model evaluation under TP-TPP framework with multiple evaluation metrics

MAPE.interpretation: In TPP prediction framework, even at prediction step 6, ED-SLSTM controls MAPE within 5%, indicating high short-term prediction accuracy

R².interpretation: In TP framework at prediction step 6, R² remains above 0.85, proving long-term trend prediction effect meets requirements

RMSE.value: closer to real value than comparison models
RMSE.interpretation: ED-SLSTM shows smaller error between real and predicted values compared to LightGBM, LSTM, Bi-LSTM, LSTM-Attention, and CEEMDAN-LSTM

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. ED-SLSTM model demonstrates higher prediction accuracy and better prediction effect than comparison models, with evaluation indices closer to real values. The model successfully addresses low prediction accuracy and long-term prediction limitations for coal mine gas concentration prediction, providing technical support for gas accident early warning.
